calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Nov-28-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Andrew Shultz appeared via zoom for Daniel Cunnigham (Petitioner).
Shawn Jackson (beneficiary) appeared via zoom.

Discussion was held regarding Mr. Jackson consenting to the termination of the Court's supervision, that he understands and consents to the Court supervision and exoneration of the trustee's bond, and that he is being supported by the County of San Francisco. The matter is taken under submission with the intent to grant as prayed.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Cabrera. Not reported.

Appearances: Andrew Schultz, via ZOOM, for Daniel Cunningham, via ZOOM, Trustee.

The court requests an update regarding the status of an accounting. Mr. Schultz reports that he received schedules from his client yesterday and that some revisions are required but that he expects that an accounting should be filed soon.

The Future Accounting is continued to Nov-02-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Ms. Alavi stated the Mr. Cunningham is working on the accounting, but he is not quite done, that he had some medical issues, but is now doing better, and requested a 30-day continuance. Status on filing of the second accounting is continued to October 5, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-28-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with Daniel Cunningham.

The Court raised the issue of the expenses that weren't supported by receipts, the case manager invoices having very little information in them and questions the expense for $160.00, the junk removal, the dish and whether some of these expenses should be borne by others who benefitted, and whether Mr. Cunningham had authority to pay Mariya Jackson for case management services. Mr. Schultz stated that the $160.00 was for cigarettes, that they made it clear that this was not an appropriate reimbursement, that the expenses for the removal of junk was entirely Shawn's expense and that the Brother had already moved out. Mr. Schultz also stated that the Brother and the Niece thought that the trust would be paying half of the rent, that the trust couldn't and that they made an agreement for Shawn to pay the total costs for the dish. In addition, Mr. Schultz spoke of Shawn's mental health condition, stated that there are behavioral issues and it was difficult to find a Case Manager that Shawn would accept, that a Trustee generally hires a Case Manager without seeking authorization from the Court, that it is routine, that Mr. Cunningham didn't think he had to come back to Court and that he did not think of it as a third party. Furthermore, Mr. Schultz stated that there are a lot of personal details in the Case Manager's report that they did not provide because of confidentiality, that Mariya Jackson was hired as a family member with the intent that she would eventually become the Conservator for Shawn, but that didn't happen, and that Mr. Cunningham recently terminated Ms. Jackson. Mr. Cunningham stated that Mariya is keeping in touch with him and he is in touch with APS, that Mariya's Father was assisting her, but is no longer providing assistance and he was not sure Mariya was doing everything that was needed for Shawn, that he will be making a visit on Wednesday or Thursday and check in with APS, and that they will have discussion about the need for a conservatorship. The First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
